envelope_assembly: Project-specific; durable waterfront/institutional envelope where applicable

sources.title: Time-Saver Standards for Building Types — Aquariums
Smithsonian exhibition planning guidance (used for public-exhibit planning logic only)
AZA accreditation/resources (used conceptually for animal care and operations; no dimensional standards embedded)

small.name: Regional Aquarium
small.notes: Lobby, ticketing, small galleries, life support rooms

medium.name: New England Aquarium
medium.notes: Major public aquarium with central tank and support systems

large.name: Georgia Aquarium
large.notes: Large destination aquarium with multiple major habitats

space_library.name: Lobby / Ticketing / Orientation
notes: High-volume queuing and visitor orientation zone.

space_library.name: Exhibit Gallery / Visitor Path
notes: Sequence, sightlines, and controlled lighting are primary planning drivers.
Major tank geometry is project-specific and intentionally not over-prescribed here.

space_library.name: Touch Tank / Interactive Zone
notes: Requires intensive operations, hygiene, and life-support coordination.

space_library.name: Education Classroom / Program Room
notes: Support school groups and public programs.

space_library.name: Gift Shop / Retail
notes: Often placed on visitor arrival or exit path.

space_library.name: Cafe Support / Pantry
notes: Populate later if food service becomes core program.

space_library.name: Life Support / Water Treatment
notes: Critical aquarium-specific infrastructure; area varies widely by tank count and water volume.

space_library.name: Quarantine / Animal Care Support
notes: Secure BOH animal-care zone; detailed husbandry planning deferred.

space_library.name: Loading / Service
notes: Separate from public path; supports exhibits, life support, and food service.

space_library.name: Public Restrooms
notes: Fixture counts deferred to later code pass.
